questions.question: When were the final NIST PQC standards (FIPS 203, 204, 205) published?
questions.explanation: FIPS 203, 204, and 205 were officially published on August 13, 2024.

questions.question: How many submissions did NIST receive in Round 1?
questions.explanation: NIST received 82 complete submissions by November 2017.

questions.question: What happened to SIKE in August 2022?
questions.explanation: SIKE was completely broken by a polynomial-time classical attack.

questions.question: How many security categories did NIST define?
questions.explanation: NIST defined 5 security categories (1-5), with Category 5 being equivalent to AES-256.

questions.question: What is SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) based on?
questions.explanation: SLH-DSA is based on SPHINCS+, a stateless hash-based signature scheme.

questions.question: The NIST PQC standardization process took 8 years from the 2016 call to the 2024 final standards.
questions.explanation: The process began in December 2016 and concluded with final standards in August 2024.
